tasks.name: Pilot H3: Replication with null controls
tasks.description: Confirm H3 REVERSED finding with proper null controls. Use same 100 features from full_h3. Add: (1) shuffled feature directions (null baseline), (2) random unit vectors. Test alpha in {3, 10}. Verify: (1) null controls have near-zero effect, (2) high-absorption features still show higher sensitivity than low-absorption. Addresses evolution lesson about hedging classification bias.

pilot.pass_criteria: Null controls mean effect < 0.05 AND high-absorption effect > low-absorption effect at alpha in {3, 10}

visualizations.description: Steering effect by condition: high-absorption, low-absorption, shuffled, random
UAS vs steering sensitivity with null baseline (pilot)

tasks.notes: Critical for validating H3 is not a measurement artifact. Addresses evolution lesson: hedging classification bias. Pilot target ~10 min.

tasks.name: Full H3: Null-controlled steering replication
tasks.description: Full-scale replication of H3 reversal with proper null controls. Use same 100 features from full_h3. Test alpha in {1, 3, 5, 10, 20}. Include: shuffled directions, random unit vectors, and also test with alpha=5 specifically to replicate the original full_h3 result. Compute Spearman r for all conditions. Report 95% confidence intervals.

pilot.pass_criteria: Null controls near-zero effect AND r > 0.2 between UAS and steering sensitivity

visualizations.description: UAS vs steering sensitivity: full null-controlled replication
Effect sizes by condition and alpha

tasks.notes: ~45 min estimated due to multiple alpha values and null conditions. Addresses evolution lesson: hedging classification bias and H3 falsifiability.

tasks.name: Full H5: CounterFact/TruthfulQA downstream evaluation
tasks.description: Replace synthetic counterfactual pairs with CounterFact or TruthfulQA for causal task evaluation. Use 100 features (low/mid/high absorption). Evaluate on: (1) gender detection from names (simple), (2) TruthfulQA (causal reasoning). Compare absorption effect on both task types. Addresses evolution lesson: H5 pilot uses non-existent threshold and weak causal task.

pilot.pass_criteria: Causal task AUC > 0.55 for at least one absorption bin AND task-dependence delta > 5%

visualizations.description: Downstream accuracy by absorption bin and task type (CounterFact/TruthfulQA)

tasks.notes: Addresses evolution lesson: H5 pilot causal task has near-random discriminability (AUC ~0.5). TruthfulQA is a real causal reasoning benchmark.

tasks.name: Pilot H1: Gemma-2B absorption atlas
tasks.description: Attempt to load Gemma-2B SAEs from gemma-2b-res release. If HuggingFace gated, use Pythia-2B as fallback. Compute absorption scores at layers [4, 8, 12, 16, 20]. Quick check for layer-wise pattern.

pilot.pass_criteria: SAE loads successfully AND absorption computed for at least 3 layers

visualizations.description: Absorption score vs. layer for Gemma-2B

shared_resources.name: gemma-2-2b-it
shared_resources.path: pre-trained via HookedTransformer.from_pretrained (may require HF token)

tasks.notes: Previous full_h1_gemma actually ran on GPT-2 due to Gemma gating. Need to confirm Gemma-2B availability or use Pythia-2B.

tasks.name: Full H3: Cross-model replication on Gemma-2B
tasks.description: Replicate H3 REVERSED finding on Gemma-2B layer 12. Use same protocol as full_h3: 100 features (50 high UAS, 50 low UAS), multiple alpha values. Confirm that the positive correlation between UAS and steering sensitivity holds across model families.

pilot.pass_criteria: Spearman r > 0.2 between UAS and steering sensitivity on Gemma-2B

visualizations.description: UAS vs steering sensitivity on Gemma-2B
GPT-2 vs Gemma-2B steering sensitivity comparison

tasks.notes: Addresses evolution lesson: H3 should be replicated across model families. Gemma-2B confirmation strengthens the central finding.

tasks.name: Ablation: Hub feature interpretation for H3
tasks.description: Investigate WHY high-absorption features are more steerable. Compute activation frequency, decoder norm, and cosine similarity with top-5 similar features for each of the 100 features in full_h3. Test hypothesis: absorbed features are hub-like (high activation frequency, high similarity with many others). Regression analysis: steering effect ~ activation_frequency + decoder_norm + cos_sim_variance.

visualizations.description: Feature properties: activation frequency vs decoder norm vs steering effect

tasks.notes: Addresses evolution lesson: paper claims absorbed features are hub-like but mechanism is proposed, not validated. This ablation tests the hub interpretation directly.

tasks.name: Synthesis: Compile results for paper writing
tasks.description: Compile all experimental results into a unified results summary. Create: (1) main results table, (2) figure generation scripts, (3) statistical significance summaries, (4) effect size comparisons. Generate paper-ready figures and tables.

visualizations.description: All main results in paper-ready format

tasks.notes: This is the final data compilation step before writing. Should produce all figures and tables needed for the paper.

notes: Next iteration focus: (1) H3 null control replication to validate central finding, (2) H5 with real causal QA (TruthfulQA), (3) Gemma-2B cross-model replication, (4) hub mechanism ablation. Total estimated: ~3 GPU-hours. Key finding: H3 REVERSED — high-absorption features show HIGHER steering sensitivity (r=+0.35). Previous completed experiments: pilot_h1_h4, full_h1_gpt2, full_h2, full_h3, h4_uas_dev, full_h4, ablation_ortho_lambda, ablation_l1_sparsity, full_h5 (pending review).
